title: biscutella longifolia subsp. subaphylla
intro: Biscutella longifolia subsp. subaphylla, also known as Alpine Biscutella, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Biscutella longifolia subsp. subaphylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
